title: IRS Code 152 Meaning (I/A Entity Updated) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 152 means I/A Entity Updated. See how it interacts with codes 806, 290, and 570 – and turn the full transcript into a client-ready report.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 152 Meaning

IRS Code 152 typically means an account update was recorded (i/a entity updated). This page explains when it shows up on transcripts, what it usually signals, and practical next steps. Want to see what clients get? Open the sample report, then run your own transcript when you’re ready.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 152?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 152 typically means an account update was recorded (i/a entity updated).

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 806, 290, 570
Typical context
Processing, assessment, credit/payment activity
Client-friendly summary
"An account update was recorded (i/a entity updated)"

When does Code 152 appear?

  • Soon after a change posts: This code shows once the IRS posts that update to the account transcript.
  • During active processing: It can appear while a return, payment, credit, or notice is moving through normal processing.
  • Timing varies: Dates on the transcript matter more than the “code list.” Always read the sequence and amounts.

What does Code 152 usually signal?

If this appears alone

It usually means the update posted, but the transcript may not show the next step yet. Pull the transcript again after the next cycle if you expect additional activity.

If this appears with Code 806 or 766/768

Treat this as part of a refund story. Compare posting dates and amounts to confirm whether money is being applied, issued, or moved.

If this appears with Code 290 or 300

Treat this as part of a balance-due story. Review assessments, payments, and penalties to confirm what changed and what is still owed.

If this appears with Code 570

Treat this as part of a hold story. A hold often means the account needs matching, verification, or an internal check before the next step posts.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Read the line in context: Use the date, amount, and nearby codes to understand what changed and what happened next.
  2. Cross-check client facts: Compare the transcript to what the client did (filed, paid, received a notice, requested a change).
  3. Send a one-paragraph update: Summarize what posted, what it likely means, and the next action (wait, pay, respond, or investigate).

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how Code 152 appears in a client-ready transcript report with context:

Transcript Processing Timeline
Code 152 – An account update was recorded (i/a entity updated)
Date: 03/15/2024
Code 806 – Refund applied to next year
Amount: $2,450
Code 571 – Refund adjustment
Reason: Estimated tax payment reconciliation

FAQ

What does IRS Code 152 mean?

IRS Code 152 typically means an account update was recorded (i/a entity updated). The meaning is confirmed by reading the posting date, amount, and surrounding transcript entries.

Is Code 152 enough to determine refund status?

Not by itself. Refund outcomes depend on credits, holds, offsets, and issuance entries. Use the sequence and amounts to see what happened next.
